inputs.command:
cd vendor/get-anything-done && git add bin/ lib/ && git status --short && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(trace): phase 25 milestone A — trace schema v4 + hook handler + gad install hooks

Ships the hook-captured event stream foundation for trace schema v4.
Tasks 22-34 (install.js fix) and 25-01/02/03/04 (phase 25 milestone A).

22-34: install.js path resolution fix
  bin/install.js expected vendor/get-anything-done/get-anything-done/ — a
  subdirectory that only exists in npm-published tarballs, not local git
  checkouts. Before crashing it deleted .claude/commands/gad/ and
  .claude/hooks/statusline.js. Fix: existence check before copying the
  get-anything-done skill, and ensure the CHANGELOG/VERSION destination
  parent exists even when the skill copy was skipped. Verified by running
  the installer for --claude --cursor --codex --local to completion.

25-01: lib/trace-schema.cjs
  Event envelope + 4 event type factories + type guard + JSONL parser.
  Exports TRACE_SCHEMA_VERSION=4, EVENT_TYPES constants, makeToolUseEvent,
  makeSkillInvocationEvent, makeSubagentSpawnEvent, makeFileMutationEvent,
  isTraceEvent, parseTraceEventsJsonl. Validated via node REPL smoke test.

25-03: lib/trace-truncate.cjs
  4 KB head+tail truncation helper (decision gad-60). truncateOutput splits
  at 2 KB head + 2 KB tail with a `... [truncated N bytes] ...` marker.
  truncateInputsObject applies the same to object field values. Multibyte-
  safe via Buffer-level slicing. Returns { value, truncated, originalBytes }.

25-02: bin/gad-trace-hook.cjs
  Claude Code PreToolUse / PostToolUse handler. Reads hook payload from
  stdin, finds project root by walking up for .planning/, reads the
  .planning/.trace-active-skill marker, detects skill transitions against
  .planning/.trace-last-skill, and writes trace events to
  .planning/.trace-events.jsonl.

  PreToolUse: checks for skill transitions only (lets the transition event
  land before the tool use that triggered the hook). PostToolUse: emits a
  tool_use event plus, when app
... [truncated 20 bytes] ...
spawn (for Task tool
  invocations) and/or file_mutation (for Write/Edit/NotebookEdit).

  Never blocks the main agent — all errors are logged to stderr and the
  hook exits 0. Verified end-to-end with a mock PostToolUse payload: the
  hook correctly appended a valid tool_use event to the JSONL file.

25-04: gad install hooks CLI subcommand
  New `gad install` + `gad uninstall` subcommands in bin/gad.cjs.
  `gad install hooks` writes PreToolUse + PostToolUse references to the
  framework-versioned handler path into .claude/settings.json (local) or
  ~/.claude/settings.json (--global). Merges into existing hooks — does
  not overwrite the user's other entries. Existing GAD trace entries are
  filtered out before re-adding so re-running the command is idempotent.
  `gad uninstall hooks` is the reverse operation.

  `gad install all --claude --cursor --codex --local` delegates to
  bin/install.js for the full framework install (wrapping the GSD-pattern
  installer). Locks in task 22-33 as satisfied by delegation.

  Verified: `gad install hooks` successfully merged the GAD trace handler
  into an existing .claude/settings.json that already had 8 hook entries;
  all pre-existing hooks preserved.

Smoke test verified:
  - trace-schema: event factories produce valid JSON, type guard round-
    trips, TRACE_SCHEMA_VERSION=4
  - trace-truncate: small inputs passthrough, 5000-byte input truncates
    to 4127 bytes (head + marker + tail), truncated flag set
  - gad-trace-hook: appends valid tool_use event to
    .planning/.trace-events.jsonl when piped a mock PostToolUse payload
  - gad install hooks: merges into settings.json without overwriting

Phase 25 milestone A is complete. Milestone B (skill_invocation marker
contract + subagent_spawn wiring + file_mutation emission) still needs
documentation updates in skills to tell agents to write .trace-active-skill,
and the full end-to-end test of a real eval run against the new hooks.

Made-with: Claude Opus 4.6
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5 && git push origin main 2>&1 | tail -3

inputs.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: phase 25 milestones B/C/D + phase 27 track 3 — autonomous batch

Phase 25 Milestone B — skill marker docs:
  - 25-07: Added "Trace marker contract" section to skills/create-skill/SKILL.md
    explaining .planning/.trace-active-skill usage (write skill id at start,
    clear at end; hook handler detects transitions and emits events).
  - Updated plan-phase, execute-phase, verify-phase with trace marker notes.
  - 25-08/09/10 were already shipped in milestone A's gad-trace-hook.cjs:
    skill_invocation on .trace-active-skill transitions, subagent_spawn on
    Task tool calls, file_mutation on Write/Edit/NotebookEdit.

Phase 25 Milestone C — framework versioning:
  - 25-12: lib/framework-version.cjs + top-level `gad version [--json]`
    command. Reads package.json version + git rev-parse HEAD/branch/commit-ts
    from the GAD repo root. Returns { version, commit, branch, commit_ts, stamp }.
  - 25-13: evalTraceInit now stamps framework_version, framework_commit,
    framework_branch, framework_commit_ts, framework_stamp, and
    trace_schema_version=4 into every new TRACE.json.
  - 25-14: evalDiff (score-based) now checks framework_commit across two
    runs BEFORE showing the diff table. If they differ, emits a prominent
    "FRAMEWORK MISMATCH" warning explaining that score deltas may reflect
    framework changes, not agent changes. If either run is unstamped (pre-v4),
    warns that cross-version comparisons are unreliable.

Phase 25 Milestone D — site prebuild for trace v4:
  - 25-15: build-site-data.mjs extended to parse trace_schema_version,
    framework fields (version/commit/branch/ts/stamp), and traceEvents
    array from v4 TRACE.json files. All new fields on EvalRunRecord.
    Backwards-compatible: v3 runs get traceSchemaVersion=3, nulls for
    framework fields, null for traceEvents. Site rendering updates
    (tool use timeline component, subagent spawns section, framework
    badge on per-run page) deferred to the first session that has a
    real v4 run to render — the data pipeline is ready, the UI components
    come when there's data to show.

Phase 27 Track 3 — derived metrics:
  - 27-11: build-site-data.mjs computes derived metrics per run:
    divergence_score (|composite - human_review|), plan_adherence_delta
    (tasks_planned - tasks_completed), produced_artifact_density (artifacts
    per minute), total_commits, commit_discipline (task_id_commits /
    total_commits). Stubbed: tool_use_mix, skill_to_tool_ratio,
    subagent_utilization (require trace v4 events from phase 25 milestone A
    hooks running on a real eval).
  - EvalRunRecord type gains `derived` object exported in generated TS.
  - 27-12 (render derived metrics on per-run page) and 27-13/14 (charts)
    deferred to the next session's UI pass — the data pipeline is live,
    rendering follows.

Build output: 158 pages. Generated data now includes framework version
stamps and derived metrics for every implementation run.

Made-with: Claude Opus 4.6
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5 && git push origin main 2>&1 | tail -3

inputs.command:
cd /c/Users/benja/Documents/custom_portfolio/vendor/get-anything-done && git add .planning/ evals/ site/ && git status --short | wc -l && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
eval: round 4 greenfield partial — rate-limited, preserved + findings doc

Round 4 v4 greenfield attempt #1. All three conditions (GAD v9, Bare v4,
Emergent v3) hit an account-level rate limit simultaneously at ~14 minutes.
None of the runs reached their gates. Partial data captured and honest.

Preserved runs:
- evals/escape-the-dungeon/v9/         — GAD, 81 tool_uses, full planning suite
  (ROADMAP/TASK-REGISTRY/STATE/DECISIONS/VERIFICATION — 7 phases, 23 tasks)
  + working dist (phase 01 verified PASS)
- evals/escape-the-dungeon-bare/v4/    — Bare, 45 tool_uses, flat worklog.md
  plan, 6 source files, manually built dist (54 KB)
- evals/escape-the-dungeon-emergent/v3/ — Emergent, 40 tool_uses, 7 inherited
  skills + 6 modular source files but NO main.ts — build fails objectively
  at vite link ("Rollup failed to resolve import /src/main.ts")

TRACE.json for each marked with:
- trace_schema_version: 4
- framework_stamp: v1.32.0+3ef0bb5 (first stamped runs!)
- timing.rate_limited: true + rate_limit_note
- human_review.notes explicitly says "DO NOT include in cross-round comparisons
  against completed runs"
- All scores and gate verdicts derived from observed partial state (not
  hallucinated — computed from what actually landed on disk)

Two new dists copied into site/public/playable/:
- escape-the-dungeon/v9/     — GAD title screen (only gate-verifiable work)
- escape-the-dungeon-bare/v4/ — bare scaffold + partial combat UI (manually
  built because agent was cut off before running npm run build)
- escape-the-dungeon-emergent/v3/ — NOT PLAYABLE (build fails; entry missing)

FINDINGS-2026-04-09-round-4-partial.md:
- Full write-up of what each condition accomplished in 14 minutes
- Cross-condition planning differential table (GAD 23 tasks planned vs Bare 10
  vs Emergent 0) — the planning overhead of GAD is clearly visible even in
  the truncated window
- Architectural differential: GAD subdirectory split, Bare flat file, Emergent
  state-composition influenced module structure
- Explicit list of what this data does NOT tell us (freedom hypothesis neither
  confirmed nor refuted, v4 still untested, trace events not yet processed)
- Retry plan: serial execution when rate limit resets

Decisions:
- gad-62: Parallel eval runs share one account rate bucket. Serial execution
  is the new default. Parallel requires documented per-run budget.
- gad-63: Rate-limited runs are preserved as data points but explicitly
  excluded from cross-round quality comparisons. Filter key is
  timing.rate_limited === true.

Site prebuild: 23 runs (was 20), 10 playable builds (was 8). New per-run
pages auto-generated for v9 / v4 / v3. New finding doc auto-indexed at
/findings/2026-04-09-round-4-partial. No UI code changes this commit — just
data. Filter-out-rate-limited logic for Graphs + Results is a follow-up task
for the next session.

Framework: v1.32.0+3ef0bb5 (trace hooks installed, framework versioning live).
These are the first three TRACE.json files stamped with framework_commit —
cross-version comparisons from here on can distinguish "agent improved" from
"framework changed."

Made-with: Claude Opus 4.6
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5 && git push origin main 2>&1 | tail -3

feat(site): filter rate-limited runs from cross-round comparisons (gad-63)

New isRateLimited(run) helper + comparableRuns() wrapper in lib/eval-data.ts.
Applied consistently across three surfaces:

- Graphs.tsx scatter + bar chart: runsWithScores() now excludes
  isRateLimited runs from both the composite×human review scatter and the
  per-run bar chart. Freedom-hypothesis signal stays clean.
- Results.tsx cards: DISPLAY_RUNS filters isRateLimited in addition to
  requiring humanReview.score. The grid of per-run result cards no longer
  shows the round 4 partial runs.
- Per-run page: new amber "rate-limited · excluded from cross-round
  comparisons" badge in the header row, plus a full callout block above
  the divergence callout that explains the exclusion, cites gad-63, and
  links to /findings/2026-04-09-round-4-partial. Divergence callout now
  suppressed when rateLimited to avoid double-warning overload.

Runs are still preserved on disk and reachable at /runs/<project>/<version>
for archival purposes — only the aggregation surfaces filter them out.
The rate_limit_note from TRACE.json timing is surfaced verbatim in the
per-run callout so readers see exactly why the run was flagged.

Build verified clean: 23 per-run pages still generate (8 comparable runs
filtered from the cards + 3 rate-limited now hidden from scatter +
20 historical + 3 rate-limited = all 23 still have pages, just not on
aggregate surfaces).

inputs.new_string:
<decision id="gad-65">
    <title>Compound-Skills Hypothesis — emergent workflow as a skill-growth framework tailored to a specific project domain</title>
    <summary>The emergent eval condition is not just "inherit some skills and don't regress." It is a testable hypothesis about knowledge compounding: **as an emergent agent iterates on the same project domain across rounds, its inherited skill library should grow more specialized to that domain, and the game it produces should improve in quality and requirements-alignment per round more than bare produces by starting fresh.** The craftsman metaphor: a blacksmith who learns one skill per hour and authors one skill per hour becomes a master of the specific kind of blade they keep forging. The test signals: (1) human review score increases monotonically across emergent versions; (2) inherited skill count grows across rounds; (3) the ratio of project-specific skills to generic methodology skills trends toward specific; (4) time-to-working-game decreases across rounds despite the requirements getting stricter. Round 4 emergent v4 produced the first observed full inheritance → apply → evolve → document cycle (authored dom-over-kaplay + pressure-forge-coupling and deprecated kaplay-scene-pattern). The next emergent run (v5) is the first real test of whether v4's learnings actually help. DISTINCT from the freedom hypothesis (bare beats GAD on creative implementation) — CSH is specifically about whether the emergent mechanism produces compounding returns over time, independent of the bare vs GAD framing.</summary>
    <impact>Phase 27 rubric adds a sixth dimension for emergent runs: skill_inheritance_effectiveness (weight 0.20), which directly scores whether a run productively applied inherited skills, evolved them, and authored new skills worth inheriting. Site's /projects/escape-the-dungeon-emergent page gains a skill lineage view showing which skills each run inherited, evolved, deprecated, and authored. Findings writeups track CSH metrics separately from freedom-hypothesis metrics. Every emergent run's TRACE.json should capture inherited_skill_count, authored_skill_count, deprecated_skill_count, and (optionally) a specificity_score measuring how tailored the authored skills are to the roguelike domain vs generic workflow.</impact>
  </decision>

  <decision id="gad-64">
    <title>API-interrupted runs are a separate failure category from rate-limited runs</title>

name: find-sprites
description: Source visual assets (sprites, icons, tilesets, portraits) for a game or UI-heavy build in a way that yields a coherent, intentional look instead of a debug-console aesthetic. Use this skill when you need art for rooms, entities, UI controls, HP bars, spell icons, status effects, or any other visual element; when the build is failing its UI-quality gate because it looks unintentional; or when you're about to fall back to raw ASCII/text UI. The goal is "looks like someone designed it" — not photorealism, not 1:1 with AAA games, but internally consistent and readable.
---

# find-sprites

Agents left to their own devices tend to ship raw text UI, emoji salad, or inconsistent asset mixtures. This skill exists because the `ui-quality` gate consistently fails when asset sourcing is treated as an afterthought. Source assets deliberately, in preference order, and commit to one visual direction.

## Preference order

Work down this list. Only drop to the next tier if the current tier cannot cover your need.

### Tier 1 — Installable icon and sprite packages

Lightweight, well-typed, import cleanly. Best for UI iconography, status effects, resource icons, and action buttons.

| Package | Use for |
|---|---|
| `lucide-react` / `lucide` | UI icons, buttons, controls, status (~1500 icons) |
| `@iconify/react` / `@iconify-json/*` | Broadest set — game-icons pack, emojione, twemoji, etc. |
| `@tabler/icons` | Clean outline style, good for minimalist UI |
| `heroicons` | Simple outline + solid, Tailwind-friendly |

For game iconography specifically, **`@iconify-json/game-icons`** (Delapouite/Lorc) is the single highest-leverage install:

```sh
npm install @iconify/react @iconify-json/game-icons
```

```tsx
import { Icon } from "@iconify/react";
<Icon icon="game-icons:crossed-swor
... [truncated 21374 bytes] ...
ntentionally.** Only acceptable for background panels or temporary placeholders you will replace before shipping.

### Tier 5 — Raw ASCII / text-only

This is a gate failure. Do not ship text-only UI. If you are tempted to, go back to Tier 1 and install the game-icons pack — it takes 30 seconds.

## Coherence over polish

One consistent visual direction beats a mix of high-quality assets from different styles. A game built entirely from Kenney's 1-bit pack looks intentional. A game mixing hand-drawn portraits, pixel monsters, and flat-vector UI icons looks broken — even if each individual asset is beautiful.

Before sourcing anything, decide:

1. **Perspective** — top-down, side-view, or UI-only?
2. **Fidelity** — pixel, vector, or illustrated?
3. **Palette** — dark fantasy, bright cartoon, monochrome?

Then source only assets matching those three answers. If a must-have asset doesn't match, either adapt it (recolor, resize) or redraw the rest of your direction to match it.

## Licensing

Always check the license. CC0 is the simplest — no attribution required, no restrictions. CC-BY requires attribution (include the author in `LICENSES.md`). Avoid CC-BY-NC (non-commercial) and CC-BY-SA (share-alike viral) unless you've confirmed your project's distribution model is compatible. Never ship assets of unknown provenance.

## Failure modes

- **Mixing styles.** Pixel + vector + illustrated = incoherent. Pick one.
- **Installing an icon pack and never using it.** The install is the easy part. Actually replace placeholder text with `<Icon />` in every UI surface.
- **Downloading a tileset without a license check.** Ship-blocker if the license forbids the use.
- **Deferring art to "after mechanics work."** The UI-quality gate is scored on what actually ships. An unpolished build with working mechanics still fails the gate.
- **Using emoji as the entire UI.** Reads as a debugger, not a game.

inputs.old_string: <decision id="gad-68">
inputs.new_string:
<decision id="gad-71">
    <title>data/ JSON pseudo-database for hand-curated site content</title>
    <summary>User proposal: "we might want to have a pseudo database that is json some json that gets stored in the repo and we use that to collect all the static data we need for the site and operate like it is a database, just in repo as dont expect customers and sensitive information at all." Formalize the pattern: `vendor/get-anything-done/data/*.json` files are the authoritative source of hand-curated content for the site. The prebuild script (`site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs`) reads them at build time and emits typed TypeScript via `site/lib/*.generated.ts`. The site never runs queries at request time — every page is SSG, every data file is read once at prebuild, types are statically exported. This is distinct from `.planning/` XML (which is agent-authored process state like phase/task/decision) and from `evals/<project>/<version>/TRACE.json` (which is eval-run output). `data/` is specifically for curated knowledge that doesn't belong in either of those two places: open questions for the public, observed bugs across eval runs, glossary terms, user-workflow assumptions, content packs extracted for gad-66, human-review tracking metadata, etc. Security posture is explicit: this is a static site with no customers, no authentication, no PII, no secrets. JSON-in-git is appropriate because we have no need for ACID, transactions, or concurrent writers — one human edits files in a branch, the prebuild regenerates, the site ships. If we ever need any of those properties (we don't expect to), the migration path is clear: JSON schemas become table definitions, the prebuild becomes a seed script.</summary>
    <impact>(1) Create `vendor/get-anything-done/data/` with an initial set of files: `open-questions.json`, `bugs.json`, `glossary.json`, `assumptions.json`. (2) Extend `build-site-data.mjs` with a new section that reads each data file, validates shape, and writes corresponding TS exports. (3) New site routes backed by these files: `/questions` (open-questions.json), eventually `/glossary`, `/assumptions`. (4) Every file gets a top-level `_schema` field documenting its shape + a `_last_updated` field maintained by hand. (5) When a new hand-curated data type is needed, the pattern is: add `data/<name>.json` → add a reader to `build-site-data.mjs` → add a typed export to `lib/<name>.generated.ts` → build a consuming page. No new runtime services, no servers, no API routes.</impact>
  </decision>

  <decision id="gad-68">

/**
 * True when the run hit a rate limit before completing. Rate-limited runs
 * are preserved as data points but excluded from cross-round comparisons
 * per decision gad-63.
 */
export function isRateLimited(r: EvalRunRecord): boolean {
  if (!r.timing) return false;
  const flag = (r.timing as Record<string, unknown>).rate_limited;
  return flag === true;
}

/**
 * True when the run was interrupted by an Anthropic API error (e.g. HTTP 529
 * overloaded_error). Different cause than rate limit — infrastructure issue
 * rather than account cap — but same exclusion policy per decision gad-64.
 */
export function isApiInterrupted(r: EvalRunRecord): boolean {
  if (!r.timing) return false;
  const flag = (r.timing as Record<string, unknown>).api_interrupted;
  return flag === true;
}

/**
 * True when the run was interrupted for ANY reason (rate limit OR API error).
 * Use this as the default filter for comparison surfaces.
 */
export function isInterrupted(r: EvalRunRecord): boolean {
  return isRateLimited(r) || isApiInterrupted(r);
}

/**
 * Returns only runs suitable for cross-round comparison — completed runs
 * with a scored composite, no interruption flags.
 */
export function comparableRuns(runs: EvalRunRecord[] = EVAL_RUNS): EvalRunRecord[] {
  return runs.filter((r) => !isInterrupted(r) && r.scores.composite != null);
}

# Programmatic-Evaluation Gaps Audit

**Owner:** GAD framework maintainers.
**Anchor decision:** [gad-69](DECISIONS.xml) — every eval metric must answer "can this be collected programmatically?" before "how do we score it?". Minimize agent-self-report and human-judgment dependencies.
**Scope:** every field currently captured in `TRACE.json`, `human_review_rubric`, `derived` metrics, and hook output.
**Status:** living document. Updated each round. Ranked by **severity** (how much judgment we currently rely on) × **feasibility** (how mechanically checkable the signal actually is).
**Last updated:** 2026-04-09.

---

## Methodology

For every existing eval signal, answer three questions:

1. **Current source:** where does this number come from today? (TRACE.json field written by the agent, human rubric, derived formula over traces, hook event)
2. **Trust model:** is it agent-self-report, human-judgment, tool-observed, or computed-deterministic?
3. **Programmatic alternative:** if it's self-report or human, what tooling could collect the same signal objectively?

A gap exists when the answer to (2) is self-report or human AND the answer to (3) is "yes, this is mechanically checkable with reasonable effort."

---

## Ranked gaps

### G1 · Gate-pass verdict (G1 game-loop, G2 forge, G3 UI, G4 pressure)

- **Current source:** `requirementCoverage.gate_failed` boolean + `gate_notes` string, written by the agent self-reporting in TRACE.json, optionally reviewed by a human.
- **Trust model:** agent self-report, occasionally refuted by human playtest.
- **Programmatic alternative:** a **Playwright smoke-test harness** run against the preserved build in `apps/portfolio/public/evals/<project>/<version>/`. G1 game-loop is testable (title → new game → enter room → find exit → navigate → encounter → resolve → return). G2 forge is testable (open forge → select 2 runes → craft → see new spell in inventory). G3 UI is partially testable (assert no raw ASCII, assert bars rendered, assert icons present by CSS c
... [truncated 8423 bytes] ...
 coverage | high | medium | phase 25 milestone E (new) |
| G3 Build/test exit code | high | high | phase 25 milestone D follow-up |
| G6 Requirement coverage counts | high | medium | phase 27 track 2 (merge with G1) |
| G11 Skill-inheritance hygiene | high | high (hygiene) | phase 27 new task |
| G5 Commit rhythm sharpening | low | medium | polish |
| G7 Subagent spawn audit | medium | high | phase 25 milestone B verify |
| G8 Asset sourcing heuristic | medium | high | phase 25 milestone D follow-up |
| G9 Notification persistence | medium | high | phase 27 track 2 |
| G10 Per-tick redraw detection | medium | medium | phase 27 track 2 |
| G12 Consistency cross-check | low | high | nice-to-have |
| G4 Tool-use mix | — | — | already good, keep as reference pattern |

## Recommended next actions

1. **Pick G1 + G3 as the two load-bearing wins.** Together they replace the two flimsiest self-report signals (gate verdict and build-works) with deterministic outputs. Both are high feasibility. This is the highest ROI.
2. **Add G11's hygiene check as the CSH-measurement upgrade.** We already have the hook-captured data; we just need a checker that walks inherited-skill files + mutation events + CHANGELOG and reports hygiene %.
3. **Defer G2 until v5 requirements promotion lands** — expected triggers change with v5, no point building a checker against a moving target.
4. **G4 is the reference pattern.** Every new programmatic metric should follow its shape: trace events in, deterministic derivation out, typed field on EvalRunRecord, rendered on the per-run page with a data-provenance caption ("this score reads X from TRACE.json derived.foo").

## Data provenance convention (follows from G4)

Every metric rendered on the site must be annotated with:
- where the number comes from (TRACE field or derived formula)
- who produced it (agent self-report, hook, prebuild computation, human review)
- whether it's programmatic or not (badge)

This is the `data provenance` view the user asked for on the /roadmap page (task 88).

  <addendum version="v5" updated="2026-04-09">

    <requirement id="R-v5.01" amends="G2" title="Training via encounter, not menu">
      Affinity must increase as a side effect of casting spells in encounters, not as a
      direct menu action. Remove any "select affinity → train" menu that picks the trained
      element for the player. Introduce a Training Dummy encounter (low-XP, no-loot combat
      room type) whose only purpose is letting the player cast without risk so affinity
      rises through use. Any room with combat must produce affinity gain for the elements/
      runes the cast spell used. The forge becomes a place to forge and combine, not to
      train. Rationale: the casting of spells must be what increases rune affinity; direct
      training menus turn a discovery loop into a shopping list.
    </requirement>

    <requirement id="R-v5.02" title="Rune discovery as a gameplay loop">
      Not all runes are
... [truncated 9841 bytes] ...
ogue for the agent's skill-authoring/merge
      loop.
    </requirement>

    <requirement id="R-v5.20" title="Rune uniqueness within a single spell (bug fix)">
      A spell may not include the same rune twice in its ingredient list. Selecting rune X,
      then rune X again for the same spell must be rejected or de-selected. The same rune
      CAN appear across DIFFERENT spells in the player's spellbook — the constraint is
      per-spell, not per-player. Affinity gain must be calculated based on unique rune
      slots actually consumed. Observed bug (Bare v5): crafting with the same rune twice
      doubled that rune's affinity gain. Logged in data/bugs.json.
    </requirement>

    <requirement id="R-v5.21" title="Event-driven rendering (remove per-tick redraws)">
      All UI updates must be event-driven, not per-tick. No redraw loops firing at a
      fixed rate regardless of state changes. Observable symptom to eliminate: glitchy
      redraws on button clicks, observed across ALL round-4 builds. This is a
      perf-and-stability gate failure mode. Acceptable patterns: React reconciliation on
      state change, requestAnimationFrame scheduling only when animation is active.
    </requirement>

    <gate-impact-summary>
      v4 gates remain (G1 game-loop, G2 forge-with-ingenuity-payoff, G3 ui-quality,
      G4 pressure-mechanics). v5 amendments tighten G1 (death/continue, end-boss reachable),
      G2 (training is encounter-driven), G3 (notification lifecycle, map usability), and
      introduce new scored dimensions for inventory_and_equipment_present and
      npc_dialogue_present rather than new gates — a run missing those does not get
      gate-zeroed but takes a meaningful scored hit.
    </gate-impact-summary>

    <deferred-to-v6>
      <item>Deep evolution trees (multi-stage mutations).</item>
      <item>Rune affinity decay when unused.</item>
      <item>Multi-character party play — out of scope for escape-the-dungeon family.</item>
    </deferred-to-v6>

  </addendum>
</requirements>

## v5 — 2026-04-09

**Core shift from v4:** Playtest-driven expansion. v4 was a designer rewrite; v5 comes entirely from user play of Bare v5 (0.805 rubric), Emergent v4 (rescored to 0.885 after user beat floor 1), GAD v9 (rate-limited), and GAD v10 (api-interrupted). Everything in v4 still applies — v5 adds 21 new/amended requirements (R-v5.01..21) on top as a structured `<addendum>` section inside the same template XML.

**Changes from v4:**
- **R-v5.01** Training via encounter, not menu — affinity rises from casting, not selecting. Training Dummy encounter room type.
- **R-v5.02** Rune discovery as a gameplay loop — starter subset only, rest found in world, one rune per floor gated behind non-combat.
- **R-v5.03** Merchants with buy/sell/trade — at least one per floor, gold as tracked resource.
- **R-v5.04** NPC dialogue with branching outcomes — 3+ NPCs, 2+ branches each, choices change game state.
- **R-v5.05** Inventory/bag with grid + equippable items — weapon/off-hand/body/trinket slots affecting stats.
- **R-v5.06** Visible character sheet + skill tree — physical/combat skills separate from spells, distinct resource.
- **R-v5.07** Spell and skill loadout slots — forced specialization as a build-pressure mechanic.
- **R-v5.08** Progression sources sufficient to reach end boss (amends G1) — guaranteed mana-max / spell-power upgrade per floor.
- **R-v5.09** Save checkpoints + continue-after-death (amends G1) — Continue must never hard-brick.
- **R-v5.10** Notification lifecycle (amends G3) — auto-dismiss, clear on new game, no persistence across sessions.
- **R-v5.11** Rest rooms must offer rest — forge rooms combining Forge+Train+Rest must expose Rest as an action.
- **R-v5.12** Navigation and map usability (amends G3) — minimum 2D graph layout, one-click navigation.
- **R-v5.13** Combat model must be explicitly 
... [truncated 453 bytes] ...
tral visual navigation map with player token (stronger form of R-v5.12).
- **R-v5.18** Visual player vs enemy identity — Pokemon / Unicorn Overlord style (UO preferred).
- **R-v5.19** Spells as craftable ingredients — spells + runes both combine, procedural-but-semantic naming. Explicitly mirrors the emergent-evolution hypothesis (gad-68) as an in-game narrative analogue.
- **R-v5.20** Rune uniqueness within a single spell — bug fix for Bare v5's double-affinity exploit.
- **R-v5.21** Event-driven rendering — kill the per-tick redraw glitches observed across ALL round-4 builds.

**Scoring impact:**
- v4 gates remain (G1, G2, G3, G4). v5 amendments tighten G1 (death/continue, end-boss reachable), G2 (training is encounter-driven), G3 (notification lifecycle, map usability).
- New scored dimensions: `inventory_and_equipment_present`, `npc_dialogue_present` — not gates, but meaningful score hits if missing.
- Rubric weights unchanged from v4.

**Deferred to v6:**
- Deep evolution trees (multi-stage mutations).
- Rune affinity decay when unused.
- Multi-character party play — out of scope for escape-the-dungeon family.

**Brownfield vs greenfield:**
- Greenfield v5 applies to escape-the-dungeon, escape-the-dungeon-bare, escape-the-dungeon-emergent (same three templates all updated together).
- Brownfield v5 extensions are not yet authored — round 5 starts greenfield-first.

**Round 5 unblock:** This version is the trigger for round 5. Round 5 runs serially (gad-67) against this requirements set. HTTP 529 investigation + GAD v11 retry queued, see task registry.

**Source:** `evals/_v5-requirements-addendum.md` holds the prose version with full user rationale quotes. The XML addendum in each template is the machine-readable form.

**Decision references:** gad-65 (CSH), gad-68 (emergent-evolution), gad-71 (data/ pseudo-database for bug tracking), gad-72 (rounds framework — this is now round 5), gad-73 (fundamental skills triumvirate — the R-v5.19 spells-as-ingredients mechanic is the in-game analogue).
